heading, Assertions for Automation Testing Practice Exercises, level 2
end of heading, Should Be, level 3
should be - Asserts that a particular value is truthy or falsy. (Compatible with Cypress, Playwright, Selenium, WebdriverIO, etc.)
end of heading, Should Have, level 3
should have - Asserts that a DOM element has a certain attribute or property. (Compatible with Cypress, Playwright, Selenium, WebdriverIO, etc.)
end of heading, Should Equal, level 3
should equal - Asserts that a value is equal to a given reference value. (Compatible with Cypress, Playwright, Selenium, WebdriverIO, etc.)
end of heading, Should Match, level 3
should match - Asserts that a value matches a particular regular expression. (Compatible with Cypress, Playwright, Selenium, WebdriverIO, etc.)
end of heading, Should Contain, level 3
should contain - Asserts that a string includes a particular substring. (Compatible with Cypress, Playwright, Selenium, WebdriverIO, etc.)
end of heading, Should Include, level 3
should include - Asserts that an array or object includes a particular value. (Compatible with Cypress, Playwright, Selenium, WebdriverIO, etc.)
end of heading, Should Exist, level 3
should exist - Asserts that a DOM element exists in the DOM. (Compatible with Cypress, Playwright, Selenium, WebdriverIO, etc.)

heading, About this site (Practice Test Automation Website):, level 2
Best Practice Test Automation Website for Selenium, Playwright, Cypress, WebdriverIO and Postman : Free Web UI and REST API Examples
Designed for practicing test automation for web and API using popular tools in the market:

The site offers a variety of dummy examples for automated scenarios that cover many common situations, including:
Learn to automate login workflows and find additional examples for multi-factor authentication and OAuth.
end of listitem, level 1, position 1, set size 9
Automate password reset flows, with other examples like email verifications and security questions.
end of listitem, level 1, position 2, set size 9
Practice form validation techniques, with extra cases like dynamic form fields and validation rules.
end of listitem, level 1, position 3, set size 9
Automate interactions with date pickers and calendars, and explore more examples for time zone handling and scheduling.
end of listitem, level 1, position 4, set size 9
The drag-and-drop scenario: Implement drag-and-drop functionality, with additional examples like sortable lists and file uploads.
end of listitem, level 1, position 5, set size 9
Work with shadow DOM elements, including more complex scenarios like nested shadow DOMs and style encapsulation.
end of listitem, level 1, position 6, set size 9
Automate interactions with dialogs and popups, with further examples including custom modal windows and alert handling.
end of listitem, level 1, position 7, set size 9
Test API endpoints using Swagger, and explore other API documentation tools available on the site.
end of listitem, level 1, position 8, set size 9
A modern dummy real-world app using React technology:
Practice automation on a React-based application.
end of listitem, level 1, position 9, set size 9
This platform also provides a set of tools designed to help QA (Quality Assurance engineers) and SDETs (Software Development Engineers in Test) improve their skills:
Free CSS and XPath tools with preview capabilities:
Enhance your learning experience with additional tools for CSS selector generators, XPath validation, and more.
end of listitem, level 1, position 1, set size 3

Experiment with these tools in the provided JS editor and Mocha runner
end of listitem, level 1, position 2, set size 3
A color wheel app for fun:
Enjoy a playful way to learn about colors, and discover other creative tools available for making the learning process enjoyable.
